chunk_summary: This template standardizes elimination diet prescriptions for food allergy management in clinical settings, focusing on documentation, monitoring, and safety protocols for children with IgE and non-IgE mediated allergies.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure and components of an elimination diet prescription template for food allergy management.
Learn how to document suspected allergy phenotypes and triggers, including IgE and non-IgE mediated reactions.
Identify objective endpoints and stop rules for elimination diets to ensure accurate diagnosis and patient safety.
Recognize the importance of nutritional replacement plans and dietitian referrals in multi-food elimination scenarios.
key_facts: Elimination diet duration varies: 72 hours for FPIAP, 2–6 weeks for non-IgE GI elimination, and 8–12 weeks for EoE induction.
Objective endpoints include stool blood, vomiting frequency, weight gain velocity, sleep, and school attendance.
Stop rules recommend discontinuing elimination diets if no improvement is observed by the planned stop date.
Safety plans include IM epinephrine dosing and emergency care access for anaphylaxis management.
Dietitian referral triggers include avoidance of more than one major food group, faltering growth, food refusal, or caregiver anxiety.
reference_quote: WAO DRACMA guideline update emphasizes nutritional management strategies for cow’s milk allergy, including elimination diets and safe substitutes. (Reference 2)
document_key_topics: Monitoring and endpoints for food allergy management
Safety protocols for anaphylaxis and emergency care
Nutritional replacement and dietitian referral criteria
